perturbation.original_text: Exclusions. “Confidential Information” excludes information which: (a) is now or hereafter becomes generally known or available, through no act or failure to act on Recipient’s part; (b) Recipient independently knows at the time of receiving such information; (c) a third party hereafter furnishes to Recipient without restriction on disclosure and without breach of any confidentiality obligations; (d) Recipient has independently developed without using any Confidential Information or breaching this Agreement; or (e) Discloser gives written permission to Recipient to disclose.
perturbation.changed_text: Exclusions. “Confidential Information” excludes information which: (a) is now or hereafter becomes generally known or available, through no act or failure to act on Recipient’s part; (b) Recipient independently knows at the time of receiving such information; (c) a third party hereafter furnishes to Recipient without restriction on disclosure and without breach of any confidentiality obligations; or (e) Discloser gives written permission to Recipient to disclose.
perturbation.explanation: Removing exclusion (d), 'Recipient has independently developed without using any Confidential Information or breaching this Agreement;', creates a contradiction with Section 5: 'Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to those contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information, provided the Recipient does not violate its obligations under this Agreement.' Now, if Recipient independently develops something similar, it is not explicitly excluded from being considered Confidential Information, potentially conflicting with the freedom to independently develop as outlined in Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Rights. No rights or licenses to trademarks, inventions, copyrights, patents or any other intellectual property rights are implied or granted under this Agreement. Subject to the obligations of this Agreement, neither party will be precluded from independently developing technology or pursuing business opportunities similar to those covered by the Confidential Information disclosed hereunder. Each party retains sole discretion to assign or reassign the job responsibilities of its employees. Each party acknowledges that the other party may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from third parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to those contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information, provided the Recipient does not violate its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excluded independently developed information from the definition of "Confidential Information." The changed text removes this exclusion, creating a contradiction with the "Rights" section, which states the Recipient is not prohibited from independently developing similar technology. The removal of exclusion (d) directly conflicts with the recipient's right to independently develop technology, as now independently developed technology is not explicitly excluded, creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Ownership and Return of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) shall remain the property of Discloser. All copies shall bear all proprietary markings contained on or in the originals. Upon the request of Discloser, Recipient shall, at Recipient’s option, either (a) return such materials to Discloser, or (b) certify the destruction thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Ownership and Return of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) shall remain the property of Discloser. All copies shall bear all proprietary markings contained on or in the originals. Upon the request of Discloser, Recipient shall return such materials to Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'at Recipient’s option, either (a) return such materials to Discloser, or (b) certify the destruction thereof.', and replace it with 'Recipient shall return such materials to Discloser.', now the destruction is not an option anymore, creating a contradiction in Recipient's potential actions regarding Confidential Information and creating a stricter scenario.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership and Return of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information (including all copies thereof) shall remain the property of Discloser. All copies shall bear all proprietary markings contained on or in the originals. Upon the request of Discloser, Recipient shall return such materials to Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Recipient to either return or certify the destruction of confidential information. The changed text removes the option to certify destruction, mandating only the return of materials. The contradicted text, which is actually the same as the changed text, reinforces this mandatory return. Since the original text provided an option that the changed text removes, there is a clear contradiction as the changed text is now more restrictive than the original.

perturbation.original_text: Term and Termination. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for a period of three (3) years unless terminated sooner as set forth below. This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon thirty (30) days written notice, provided however, that Paragraphs 3, 4, 5, and 7 shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Term and Termination. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for a period of three (3) years unless terminated sooner as set forth below. This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon thirty (30) days written notice, provided however, that Paragraphs 3, 4, and 7 shall survive any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing paragraph 5 from the survival clause, a contradiction is created regarding the rights and obligations after termination. While the agreement states paragraphs 3, 4, and 7 survive, the absence of paragraph 5 now means that after termination, the clause 'Subject to the obligations of this Agreement, neither party will be precluded from independently developing technology or pursuing business opportunities similar to those covered by the Confidential Information disclosed hereunder' no longer applies. This contradicts the initial intent of allowing independent development, as now, after termination, such independent development might be restricted, even if it was permissible during the agreement's term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Rights. No rights or licenses to trademarks, inventions, copyrights, patents or any other intellectual property rights are implied or granted under this Agreement. Subject to the obligations of this Agreement, neither party will be precluded from independently developing technology or pursuing business opportunities similar to those covered by the Confidential Information disclosed hereunder. Each party retains sole discretion to assign or reassign the job responsibilities of its employees. Each party acknowledges that the other party may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from third parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to those contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information, provided the Recipient does not violate its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that paragraphs 3, 4, 5, and 7 survive termination, ensuring that the clause in paragraph 5 regarding independent development would continue to apply after the agreement ends. The changed text omits paragraph 5 from the survival clause. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text, which is paragraph 5, explicitly states that 'Subject to the obligations of this Agreement, neither party will be precluded from independently developing technology', but now, after termination, this clause no longer survives, potentially restricting independent development after termination when it was previously permitted.

perturbation.original_text: Miscellaneous. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of California. This Agreement contains the final, complete and exclusive agreement of the parties relative to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings and agreements relating to its subject matter and may not be changed, modified, amended or supplemented except by a written instrument signed by both parties. If any provision of this Agreement is found by a proper authority to be unenforceable, that provision shall be severed and the remainder of this Agreement will continue in full force and effect. Recipient hereby acknowledges and agrees that no remedy at law will afford Discloser adequate protection against, or appropriate compensation for, breach of Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that Discloser shall be entitled to specific performance of Recipient’s obligations, as well as such further equitable relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: Miscellaneous. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of California. This Agreement contains the final, complete and exclusive agreement of the parties relative to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings and agreements relating to its subject matter and may not be changed, modified, amended or supplemented except by a written instrument signed by both parties. If any provision of this Agreement is found by a proper authority to be unenforceable, that provision shall be severed and the remainder of this Agreement will continue in full force and effect. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that Discloser shall be entitled to specific performance of Recipient’s obligations, as well as such further equitable relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'Recipient hereby acknowledges and agrees that no remedy at law will afford Discloser adequate protection against, or appropriate compensation for, breach of Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement.', a contradiction is introduced. With this clause gone, the following sentence, 'Accordingly, Recipient agrees that Discloser shall be entitled to specific performance of Recipient’s obligations, as well as such further equitable relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction.', now lacks a clear justification. It's no longer explicitly stated that monetary damages are insufficient, making the right to specific performance potentially contestable. While the remedy remains, the reasoning for it is removed, creating an in-text contradiction because the entitlement to specific performance is asserted without the foundational acknowledgment of inadequacy of legal remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Miscellaneous. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of California. This Agreement contains the final, complete and exclusive agreement of the parties relative to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings and agreements relating to its subject matter and may not be changed, modified, amended or supplemented except by a written instrument signed by both parties. If any provision of this Agreement is found by a proper authority to be unenforceable, that provision shall be severed and the remainder of this Agreement will continue in full force and effect. Accordingly, Recipient agrees that Discloser shall be entitled to specific performance of Recipient’s obligations, as well as such further equitable relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'Recipient hereby acknowledges and agrees that no remedy at law will afford Discloser adequate protection against, or appropriate compensation for, breach of Recipient’s obligations under this Agreement.' This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text, which is identical to the original text, includes the acknowledgment that legal remedies are inadequate. Without this acknowledgment in the changed text, the entitlement to specific performance, 'Accordingly, Recipient agrees that Discloser shall be entitled to specific performance of Recipient’s obligations, as well as such further equitable relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction,' lacks a clear justification and is now contestable, creating a strong in-text contradiction based on the provided explanation and the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.
